description: The inward believing act by which the intellect assents to divine truth.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from the virtue of faith itself.

description: Giving to the needy as an act of mercy and, through mercy, of charity.
Passion of the irascible appetite concerning injury and vindication.
Backsliding from God, and absolutely speaking, withdrawal from the faith.
Distinct doctrinal articulation within the content of faith.
Passion moving away from an apprehended evil.
Beatitude or happiness as the perfected human end.
notes: In the pilot slice, happiness and beatitude are normalized together.

canonical_label: Beatitude of the Clean of Heart
description: Beatitude pronounced on the clean of heart.
Generic beatitude registry node reserved for later tract expansion.

aliases: those who hunger and thirst for justice
canonical_label: Beatitude of Hunger and Thirst for Justice
description: Beatitude pronounced on those hungering and thirsting for justice.
Beatitude pronounced on the meek.
Beatitude pronounced on the merciful.
Beatitude pronounced on those who mourn.
Beatitude pronounced on the peacemakers.

canonical_label: Beatitude of Those Persecuted for Justice
description: Beatitude pronounced on those persecuted for justice.

canonical_label: Beatitude of the Poor in Spirit
description: Beatitude pronounced on the poor in spirit.
Doing good to another as an act of friendship and charity.
Disparagement of divine goodness by denying what befits God or affirming what does not.
The special sin against the Holy Spirit as treated in questions 14-15.
disambiguation_notes: Do not silently merge this with ordinary blasphemy.

description: Privation of intellectual sight by willful turning away from intelligible principle.
Principal moral virtue around which others are ordered.
Theological virtue of friendship with God.
notes: Do not auto-normalize bare 'love' to charity outside clearly theological context.

description: Elective act of the will.
Practical act applying counsel and judgment to action.
Precepts whose fulfillment exceeds fallen nature without grace.
Good of the whole community rather than of a private individual alone.
Agreement of hearts directed together by charity.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from peace, though the tract closely relates them.

description: The outward verbal confession of what is held by faith.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from the symbol or creed as a textual formula.

description: Contrariety of speech in a disordered or truth-disclaiming manner.
Grace-type reserved in the pilot registry for later grace questions.
Gift of the Holy Spirit perfecting right counsel in action.
Vice opposed to fortitude.
The principal set of precepts delivered in the Old Law.
Passion moving toward an absent good.
Passion recoiling from a difficult good judged unattainable.
Sinful movement contrary to hope by judging pardon or salvation unavailable from God.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from despair as a passion in the Prima Secundae.

description: Dissent of wills contrary to the concord produced by charity.
Law-type reserved in the pilot registry for later tract expansion.
Obtuseness of intellectual penetration in spiritual matters.
The unity of the Church and its members described as an effect of charity.
Generic end or final cause as treated in the pilot slice.
Grief over a neighbor's good in a way contrary to charity.
disambiguation_notes: Question 14 also names envy of a brother's spiritual good as a sin-against-the-Spirit species; do not collapse the broader and narrower uses silently.

description: God's reason as the first rule of human willing.
Supernatural end exceeding the proportion of human nature.
Theological virtue assenting to divine truth on God's authority.
Counterfeit prudence ordered to an apparent rather than true good.
notes: Keep distinct from Prudence of the Flesh when both are named.

description: Passion shrinking from impending evil.

aliases: gift of fear of the Lord
canonical_label: Gift of Fear of the Lord
description: Gift of the Holy Spirit producing reverential fear before God.
Formal object of faith considered under divine revelation.
Dulled judgment in divine things, treated as contrary to wisdom.
The ordering role by which charity informs the acts of the virtues.
The cardinal virtue governing firmness in difficulties and dangers.
Gift of the Holy Spirit strengthening endurance in arduous good.
Correction of a brother's sin as an act of charity ordered to amendment.
The definitional friendship Aquinas attributes to charity.
Enjoyment or fruition of the attained good.
Future contingent events considered as prophetic matter.
Justice directing acts of virtue to the common good.
Generic gift category reserved for later tract expansion.
God as the object or term of theological virtue and charity.
disambiguation_notes: Extremely broad label; do not auto-resolve generic God language into narrow doctrinal claims.

description: Grace as the supernatural help needed beyond fallen nature.
notes: Generic grace remains distinct from operating and cooperating grace unless the text names the subtype.

description: Stable disposition ordering power toward act.
Passion opposed to love.
Sinful hatred opposed to the love due to God or neighbor.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from hatred as a passion treated in the Prima Secundae.

description: Species of unbelief choosing private judgment against Christian doctrine.
The Holy Spirit in relation to infused charity and the named gifts.
The theological virtue by which eternal beatitude is expected from God.
Act proper to man as rational and voluntary.
Law-type reserved in the pilot registry for later tract expansion.
Vice opposed to prudence.
Vice opposed to justice.
Intellectual appetite, that is, the will.
Vice opposed to temperance.
Act by which the will tends toward an end.
Act lacking full voluntariness through violence, fear, or ignorance.
Spiritual joy in God proceeding from charity.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from merely sensible delight or pleasure.

description: Moral virtue rendering acts and agents right in relation to another.
Cognitive grasp used here for prophecy and other intellectual acts.
Gift of the Holy Spirit for judging created things in relation to God.
Law as ordinance of reason for the common good.
notes: Generic law is kept distinct from named law-types unless the text is explicit.

description: The principal act belonging to charity inasmuch as charity loves.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from charity as virtue and from love as passion.

description: Love understood as a passion rather than the infused virtue of charity.
notes: Bare 'love' is ambiguous between passion-language and charity.

description: Merciful movement or act as treated in the charity tract.
disambiguation_notes: The tract can speak of mercy both as passion and as virtue-like act; keep evidence close to the cited passage.

description: Meritorious human action proportioned to supernatural end only through grace.
Law-type reserved in the pilot registry for later tract expansion.
Neighbor as the human object loved out of charity in God.
disambiguation_notes: Do not collapse this into enemy-love or ecclesial unity without explicit passage support.

description: Precepts specifically belonging to the New Law.
Formal and material object considered in the tract on faith.
Grace-type reserved in the pilot registry for later grace questions.
Habit ordered to operation rather than bare being.
Justice directing a person in relation to other individuals.
Passion of the soul considered under appetitive motion.
The ordered tranquility and union effected by charity.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from mere political quiet or absence of conflict.

description: Standing steadfastly in good without falling away.
Gift of the Holy Spirit inclining filial reverence toward God.
Reason as applied to action and counsel.
Generic precept registry node reserved for later tract expansion.
Precepts whose end and ordering belong to charity.

aliases: precepts of faith knowledge and understanding
canonical_label: Precepts of Faith, Knowledge, and Understanding
description: Precept material governing faith together with the connected gifts of knowledge and understanding.
Precepts or legal preambles ordering the acts of hope and fear.
Immoderate hope relying on God or self in a disordered way.
Notification applying law to those who must be ruled by it.
Prophetic knowledge and utterance regarding things beyond ordinary human ken.
notes: Prophecy in the pilot slice is kept distinct from the gifts of the Holy Spirit.

description: Right reason applied to action.
Prudence-like disposition placing ultimate end in bodily pleasure.
notes: Keep distinct from generic false prudence.

description: Reason as rule and measure of human acts.

aliases: render to each one his own
description: Proper act of justice in giving each one what is due.
Something less rightly done or said that occasions another's spiritual downfall.
disambiguation_notes: Keep active and passive scandal distinct at the annotation level when the passage requires it.

description: Scission from ecclesial unity, directly opposed to the unity effected by charity.
Tumult or antagonism between parts of one people, opposed to civic unity and peace.
Sensitive appetitive power in which passions are properly found.
Bad human act contrary to due rule and measure.
Oppressive sorrow about spiritual good, contrary to charity's joy.
Prompt watchfulness and alert execution in matters of action.
Private war or contradiction of deeds between persons.
Collected formula by which the truths of faith are proposed.
The cardinal virtue moderating attractions of pleasure.
Virtue having God as its immediate object.
Man's last or ultimate end.
notes: Keep distinct from Beatitude when a passage speaks only of end or final causality.

description: Sin opposed to faith by refusing or despising what faith proposes.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from mere negative ignorance where the faith has not been heard.

description: Gift of the Holy Spirit granting penetration into divine truth.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from prudence-part intelligence or understanding.

description: Act of applying a thing toward an intended end.
Vice considered as privative disorder contrary to virtue.
Generic virtue as good operative perfection.
notes: Do not collapse generic virtue into a specific virtue without explicit passage support.

description: Act proceeding from an intrinsic principle with knowledge of the end.
War as treated in the charity tract under the conditions of justice and peace.
disambiguation_notes: The tract distinguishes just from unjust war; do not flatten every war reference into a sinful relation.

description: The rational appetite and proximate principle of commanded human action.
Gift of the Holy Spirit perfecting higher judgment by divine things.
disambiguation_notes: Keep distinct from wisdom as an acquired or infused virtue.
